to turn back (hence, away) transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively (not necessarily with the idea of return to the starting point); generally to retreat; often adverbial, again

flags.note: נֶפֶשׁ — "soul" (Greek-influenced: non-material self) vs. "life" (the whole living person) vs. "self" (the person as subject). Hebrew nefesh is embodied, not a separate immaterial substance.
צְדָקָה — "righteousness" (moral quality) vs. "justice" (social-legal action). OT prophets use this for God's vindicating action for the oppressed; "righteousness" alone misses the social dimension.
